title: heracleum lanatum subsp. akasimontanum
intro: Heracleum lanatum subsp. akasimontanum is a subspecies of the Heracleum lanatum plant, commonly known as the woolly cow parsley or native cow parsley, which is a type of flowering plant native to East Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Heracleum lanatum subsp. akasimontanum include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to have anti-cancer properties. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in traditional cooking and as a natural dye.
